comment_text.sn1.22:1.1: To “impact” (phusati, elsewhere “contact” or “touch”) is to create harmful karma, which is the “it” that in turn harms the ones who impacts.
comment_text.sn1.22:2.1: This verse occurs with a troublesome brahmin at SN 7.4:2.1; as rejoinder to Kokālika’s attack on Sāriputta and Moggallāna at Snp 3.10:14.1 (see SN 6.10, which has the story without this verse); and at Dhp 125.

translation_text.sn1.22:1.1: “It doesn’t impact one who doesn’t impact others.
translation_text.sn1.22:1.2: It impacts one because they impact others.
translation_text.sn1.22:1.3: That’s why it impacts one who impacts,
translation_text.sn1.22:1.4: who wrongs one who’s done no wrong.”
translation_text.sn1.22:2.1: “Whoever wrongs a man who has done no wrong,
translation_text.sn1.22:2.2: a pure man who has not a blemish,
translation_text.sn1.22:2.3: the evil backfires on the fool,
